cityDNA.coreFantasy: A West-African maritime trade city where gold-sailed harbors, cargo yards, ancestor-masked citadels, roadside shrines, survey halls, lecture courts, and scrap districts make port life ceremonial and practical.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Port Sargasso is not a generic pirate port; the uploaded images show a broad sunlit harbor city with formal logistics, sacred roadside anchors, public works, scholarship, and visible lower-district repair.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Port Sargasso turns harbor commerce, ancestor authority, shrine obligation, public works, learning, and lower-district salvage into one West-African maritime civic order.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Gold sails, white and ochre stone, red awnings, blue cargo beacons, tide-drum harbor forms, ancestor masks, shrine lanterns, survey courts, lecture halls, cargo cranes, and scrap waterfronts.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A West-African-coded harbor civilization of merchants, dockworkers, shrine keepers, surveyors, scholars, sailors, and scrap-market families.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Gold sails, tide drums, ancestor masks, cargo seals, roadside shrines, votive groves, blue lamps, star navigation, and patched tenements symbolize trade under remembered duty.

canonLocks: Port Sargasso is a mature West-African maritime port city, not a lawless pirate harbor.
The Wonder is West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails.
The Beacon is Cargo Yard Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel.
The five primary districts are Warehousing / Logistics, Shrine / Sacred Place, Public Works Authority, University / Lyceum, and Slums / Informal Settlement.

The uploaded district anchors include Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard Beacon, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook, Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, and Patched Tenement Nook.
provisionalCanon: Port Sargasso's highest civic virtue is accountable exchange.
The image pass emphasizes a wealthy working port with ritual, logistics, scholarship, and lower-district pressure all visible.
Ancestor authority should appear civic and maritime, not merely decorative.
cautionFlags: Keep cargo logistics and shrine obligations visible together.
Do not let gold sails erase the slum/scrap-market district.

environment.environmentalLogic: The port treats tide, cargo, shrine obligation, public works, and repair districts as one harbor ecosystem. Water is commerce, memory, route, and risk.

forms: West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails
skyline: Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails as wonder
Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel as authority
Cargo Yard Beacon as harbor signal
lecture halls, survey towers, shrine roofs, cranes, and lower scrap waterfronts

civilization.people: Citizens are port-literate, ancestor-conscious, practical, social, and used to seeing trade, shrine obligation, public works, and scholarship in the same streets.
civilization.governance: The Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel governs through harbor records, cargo seals, ancestor obligations, shrine protections, survey permits, and public port duties.
civilization.religion: Religion appears through ancestor masks, roadside shrines, votive groves, tide-drum ceremonies, harbor blessings, and public offerings for safe passage.

streets.appearance: Broad, sunlit, and crowded: cargo courts, shrine corners, survey halls, lecture plazas, red awnings, blue beacons, ancestor-mask civic stone, and lower waterfront scrap stalls share the harbor edge.
streets.movement: Movement follows cargo routes, dock ramps, shrine streets, survey platforms, lecture stairs, market lanes, and scrap waterfront paths.
streets.lighting: Gold daylight and blue beacon light define the city; sunset turns sails and harbor stone into civic spectacle.
streets.weatherEffects: Sea glare, spray, wind, and harbor storms make survey work, cargo timing, and shrine rituals practically important.
streets.dayCycle: Cargo unloading, shrine visits, survey work, lectures, and market traffic dominate the day.
streets.nightCycle: Lantern-lit shrines, guarded warehouses, lower-district repairs, and harbor watch dominate night.

housing.wealthy: Citadel- and cargo-house residences with gold-sail views, carved masks, shaded courtyards, and private docks.
housing.middle: Merchant, sailor, surveyor, and scholar homes above shops or near harbor stairs.
housing.poor: Lower waterfront patched tenements, repair sheds, crowded rooms near scrap markets, and shared access to docks and public water.

districts.name: Warehousing / Logistics
districts.civicPurpose: Cargo movement, inventory control, harbor signal response, and trade accountability.
districts.visualIdentity: Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard Beacon, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, crates, cranes, and dock traffic.

districts.name: Shrine / Sacred Place
districts.civicPurpose: Roadside offerings, harbor blessings, ancestor obligation, and public passage rites.
districts.visualIdentity: Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook, lanterns, small offerings, and harbor approaches.

districts.name: Public Works Authority
districts.civicPurpose: Harbor surveys, permit enforcement, maintenance, and sea-route safety.
districts.visualIdentity: Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, water maps, work crews, and civic instruments.

districts.name: University / Lyceum
districts.civicPurpose: Public learning, star navigation, maritime scholarship, and civic instruction.
districts.visualIdentity: University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, students, maps, and water-facing plazas.

districts.name: Slums / Informal Settlement
districts.civicPurpose: Lower waterfront survival, repair culture, informal trade, and visible cost of harbor wealth.
districts.visualIdentity: Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, repair stalls, scrap roofs, and dock-edge alleys.

wonder.name: West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails
wonder.symbolicRole: Trade, tide, rhythm, sail wealth, and ancestral harbor identity made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Wonder-scale gold-sailed harbor complex with tide-drum forms, ceremonial water, and broad civic approaches.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide ceremonies, harbor festivals, trade rites, departure blessings, and public assemblies.
storyHooks: A tide-drum rhythm signals a cargo truth before the ledgers do.

beacon.name: Cargo Yard Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Trade accountability and public warning at the harbor's working heart.
beacon.visualRole: Blue-lit beacon rising from cargo yards, warehouse courts, cranes, and inventory halls.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates cargo movement, harbor alerts, public works, shrine responses, university notices, and lower-district traffic.
storyHooks: A Beacon change freezes the harbor and exposes who controls the inventory halls.

seatOfPower.name: Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Ancestral memory and maritime authority sealed into government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Water-facing citadel with ancestor masks, gold seals, tide records, and formal harbor terraces.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs cargo records, harbor law, ancestral obligations, public works authority, and petitions.
storyHooks: An ancestor mask forces the Citadel to honor a forgotten passage debt.

majorLandmarks.name: Roadside Shrine
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Sacred pause before movement and exchange.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Small shrine with lantern monument, votive grove, carved detail, and road-harbor edge placement.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Offerings, blessings, departure rites, and public memory.
storyHooks: A votive token proves someone passed through before the cargo record says they did.

majorLandmarks.name: Survey Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Survey hall with blue lamp, permit yard, instruments, maps, and harbor views.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Public works, permits, tide surveys, and route safety.
storyHooks: A survey map contradicts the cargo house's profitable route.

majorLandmarks.name: Lecture Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Lecture court with star navigation monument, students, open water, and civic terraces.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Education, navigation training, and public interpretation.
storyHooks: A student reading of the stars exposes a false harbor schedule.

majorLandmarks.name: Scrap Market
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Survival and repair beneath harbor wealth.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Patchwork waterfront market of scrap stalls, lantern monument, patched tenements, and repair activity.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Repair culture, lower-district economy, and overlooked evidence.
storyHooks: The scrap market repairs the part the cargo house officially lost.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Warehousing / Logistics Civic Hall, Cargo Yard Beacon, Warehousing Logistics Gold-Seal Monument, Inventory Hall Nook, crates, cranes, and dockworkers.
S2 must show Shrine / Sacred Place Civic Hall, Roadside Shrine, Shrine Sacred Place Lantern Monument, Votive Grove Nook, votive offerings, and shrine keepers.
S3 must show Public Works Authority Hall, Survey Hall, Blue-Lamp Monument, Permit Yard Nook, maps, instruments, and harbor work crews.
S4 must show University / Lyceum Civic Hall, Lecture Hall, Star Navigation Monument, Observatory Court Nook, students, star charts, and water-facing plazas.
S5 must show Slums / Informal Settlement Civic Hall, Scrap Market, Slums Informal Settlement Lantern Monument, Patched Tenement Nook, repair stalls, scrap roofs, and lower waterfront life.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails, Cargo Yard Beacon, and Ancestor Masks Tide-Seal Citadel legible together.
Use gold sails, cargo yards, shrines, public works halls, lecture courts, ancestor masks, blue lamps, and lower waterfront scrap markets.
requiredVisualElements: West-African Tide-Drum Harbor of Gold Sails
